description: Key syntax and usage differences between PFC 5.0 and PFC 7.0+. This guide helps migrate datafiles and FISH scripts from the PFC 5.0 syntax to the PFC 7.0+ command syntax, or understand legacy datafiles that use PFC 5.0 conventions.

sections.heading: Coordinate Axes Keywords
sections.content:
PFC 5.0 uses bare coordinate keywords (x, y, z) with values following the keyword. PFC 7.0+ uses hyphenated position keywords.

PFC 5.0:
  wall generate plane dip 90 dd 90 position 0 0 0
  ball generate radius 1.0 1.4 box -5.0 5.0 x -5.0 5.0 y -5.0 5.0 z

PFC 7.0+:
  wall generate plane dip 90 dd 0 position-x 0 position-y 0 position-z 0
  ball generate radius 1.0 1.4 box -5.0 5.0 position-x -5.0 5.0 position-y -5.0 5.0 position-z -5.0 5.0

Note: In PFC 5.0, the 'z' keyword used after the box extent (for ball generate) is a shorthand for the z-range, but the full 'x', 'y', 'z' keywords are also supported in some contexts. PFC 7.0+ requires the full position-x/position-y/position-z keywords.

sections.heading: Velocity Keywords
sections.content:
PFC 5.0 uses concatenated keywords (xvelocity, yvelocity, zvelocity). PFC 7.0+ uses hyphenated keywords (velocity-x, velocity-y, velocity-z).

PFC 5.0:
  wall attribute xvelocity 1.0e-4 range id 1
  ball attribute yvelocity 0.0

PFC 7.0+:
  wall attribute velocity-x 1.0e-4 range id 1
  ball attribute velocity-y 0.0

sections.heading: Contact Model Setup (CMAT)
sections.content:
PFC 5.0 uses the bare 'cmat' command. PFC 7.0+ requires the 'contact' prefix.

PFC 5.0:
  cmat default model linear
  cmat default model linear method deformability emod 1.0e9 kratio 0.0
  cmat default type ball-ball model linearpbond property ...
  cmat apply

PFC 7.0+:
  contact cmat default model linear
  contact cmat default model linear method deformability emod 1.0e9 kratio 0.0
  contact cmat default type ball-ball model linearpbond property ...
  contact cmat apply

sections.heading: Ball Generation Commands
sections.content:
PFC 5.0 ball generate uses 'number N box x1 x2 y1 y2 z1 z2' format. The range syntax for ball-related commands differs.

PFC 5.0:
  ball generate radius 1.0 1.4 number 30 box -5.0 5.0
  ball distribute porosity 0.2 radius 1.0e-3 1.5e-3 box -0.025 0.025 -0.025 0.025 -0.04 0.04

PFC 5.0 ball distribute sub-keywords use concatenated forms:
  numbin N           (vs PFC 7.0+: number-bins N)
  volumefraction f   (vs PFC 7.0+: volume-fraction f)
  gauss              (same in both)

PFC 5.0 ball distribute with multiple size fractions:
  ball distribute porosity 0.15 radius 1e-3 1.8e-3 box ... numbin 4 bin 1 radius 1e-3 1.3e-3 volumefraction 0.25 bin 2 radius 1.3e-3 1.6e-3 volumefraction 0.5 bin 3 radius 1.6e-3 1.8e-3 volumefraction 0.25

sections.heading: Wall Servo Keywords
sections.content:
PFC 5.0 uses concatenated parameter keywords in the wall servo command. PFC 7.0+ uses hyphenated keywords.

PFC 5.0:
  wall servo gainfactor 0.8 gainupdate 1 vmax 0.01
  wall servo xforce 1000.0 activate on range name 'top'

PFC 7.0+:
  wall servo gain-factor 0.8 gain-update 1 velocity-max 0.01
  wall servo force-x 1000.0 activate on range name 'top'

Keyword mapping:
  gainfactor  -> gain-factor
  gainupdate  -> gain-update
  vmax        -> velocity-max
  xforce      -> force-x
  yforce      -> force-y
  zforce      -> force-z

sections.heading: Wall History Keywords
sections.content:
PFC 5.0 uses a different word order for wall history commands. PFC 7.0+ places the type keyword first.

PFC 5.0:
  history wall xcontactforce id 1
  history wall ydisplacement id 2
  history wall zcontactforce id 3

PFC 7.0+:
  wall history force-contact-x id 1
  wall history displacement-y id 2
  wall history force-contact-z id 3

Mapping:
  wall xcontactforce  -> wall.force.contact(wp, 1)  (or 'force-contact-x')
  wall ycontactforce  -> wall.force.contact(wp, 2)  (or 'force-contact-y')
  wall zcontactforce  -> wall.force.contact(wp, 3)  (or 'force-contact-z')
  wall xdisplacement  -> wall.disp(wp, 1)           (or 'displacement-x')
  wall ydisplacement  -> wall.disp(wp, 2)           (or 'displacement-y')
  wall zdisplacement  -> wall.disp(wp, 3)           (or 'displacement-z')

sections.heading: FISH Variable Interpolation
sections.content:
PFC 5.0 uses the '@' prefix for FISH variable interpolation inside commands. PFC 7.0+ uses square brackets '[...]'.

PFC 5.0:
  command
    wall attribute xvelocity @servo_vel range id 3
  end_command

PFC 7.0+:
  command
    wall attribute velocity-x [servo_vel] range id 3
  end_command

Note: In PFC 5.0, when referencing FISH variables inside FISH (not in command blocks), the '@' prefix is NOT used. Variables are referenced directly:
  local x = ball.pos(bp, 1)   -- no @ prefix needed in FISH
  @x is only needed when interpolating FISH variables into command strings

sections.heading: Range Group Syntax
sections.content:
PFC 5.0 uses 'range set name' to reference groups by name. PFC 7.0+ uses the 'range group' keyword directly.

PFC 5.0:
  range set name 'groupname'
  ball attribute damp 0.7 range set name 'sample'

PFC 7.0+:
  range group 'groupname'
  ball attribute damp 0.7 range group 'sample'

In PFC 5.0, groups are created differently:
  range set name 'groupname'
  ... (implicitly)/or
  ball property density 2600 range set name 'sample' (creates and assigns in one step)

In PFC 7.0+, groups are explicit:
  ball group 'sample' range ...
  ball attribute density 2600 range group 'sample'

sections.heading: Domain Condition Syntax
sections.content:
PFC 5.0 uses 'condition stop/destroy' as a sub-keyword of the domain command. PFC 7.0+ uses 'condition ...' as a separate keyword chain.

PFC 5.0:
  domain extent -10.0 10.0 condition stop
  domain extent -10.0 10.0 condition destroy

PFC 7.0+:
  domain extent -10.0 10.0 condition stop
  domain extent -10.0 10.0 condition destroy

(The syntax for this particular command is similar between versions, but the available condition types may differ. PFC 5.0 supports 'stop' and 'destroy'; PFC 7.0+ adds additional condition types like 'reflect'.)

sections.heading: Summary of Key Differences
sections.content:
| Aspect               | PFC 5.0                     | PFC 7.0+                       |
|----------------------|-----------------------------|--------------------------------|
| Coordinate keywords  | x, y, z                     | position-x, position-y, pos-z  |
| Velocity keywords    | xvelocity, yvelocity, zvel  | velocity-x, velocity-y, vel-z  |
| CMAT command         | cmat default ...            | contact cmat default ...       |
| Ball generate range  | box x1 x2 y1 y2 [z]         | box x1 x2 [position-...]       |
| ball distribute bins | numbin, volumefraction      | number-bins, volume-fraction   |
| Wall servo           | gainfactor, gainupdate,vmax | gain-factor, gain-update,vel-max|
| Wall history         | history wall xcontactforce  | wall history force-contact-x   |
| FISH interpolation   | @var                        | [var]                          |
| Range group          | range set name 'g'          | range group 'g'                |
| Domain condition     | condition stop/destroy      | condition stop/destroy/reflect |

sections.heading: Additional Critical Differences
sections.content:
| Aspect                    | PFC 5.0                          | PFC 7.0+                        |
|---------------------------|----------------------------------|---------------------------------|
| Default contact model     | Null (no interaction)            | Linear (elastic-frictional)     |
| Default local damping     | 0.0 (must set explicitly)        | 0.0 (must set explicitly)       |
| wall servo target         | Force (not stress)               | Force (not stress)              |
| Contact method style      | Separate commands per method     | Sub-commands under contact      |
| Ball stress access        | ball.stress(bp, i, j)            | ball.stress(bp)[i,j]            |
| while_stepping            | Supported                        | Supported (same)                |
| Available contact models  | 10 (null,linear,linearcbond,     | 14+ (adds arrlinear,            |
|                           |  linearpbond,hertz,hysteretic,   |  lineardipole, softbond,        |
|                           |  smoothjoint,flatjoint,rrlinear, |  bilinear, springnetwork, etc.) |
|                           |  burger)                         |                                 |
| timestep scale            | set timestep scale [f]           | set timestep scale [f]          |
